title: Sculpting Trajectories with Waypoint Preferences

runtime.expected_outcome: By preferring specific mid-path observations, you can push the agent through a chosen branch even when the goal is reachable by a shorter path.

symbols.waypoint preference: temporary high logit on a mid-path observation
symbols.lambda: scalar controlling how strongly the agent detours

audiences.kid: Tell the agent it would enjoy a particular stop on the way. It'll go that way even if the shortest route skips it.
audiences.real: Adding a mid-path logit to C creates a waypoint attractor. The agent's policy posterior now prefers trajectories that pass through the waypoint.
audiences.equation: C = waypoint_logits + goal_logits. Tune lambda to control detour strength relative to the goal.
audiences.derivation: Waypoint preferences are a form of shaping: the pragmatic term becomes a sum of log-preferences each of which pulls the trajectory through its favoured observation. Standard constrained-optimisation intuition.

orchestrate.objective: Make the agent take a detour by adding a mid-path preference to C.
orchestrate.context: forked_paths; c_preference_override adds a high logit on a mid-path tile observation plus the default goal logit.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
